value.description: Covers the full new user signup flow on automationexercise.com, including account creation, login verification, and account deletion, plus boundary/negative cases.

tests.name: Happy Path - Complete New User Registration and Account Deletion

steps.description: Launch browser and navigate to automationexercise.com home page

steps.expected: Home page is visible and fully loaded
steps.description: Verify home page is visible successfully
Click on Signup / Login button in the navigation bar
Verify 'New User Signup!' heading is visible on the right signup form
Enter name in the signup form
Enter unique email address in the signup form
Click Signup button to proceed to account information page
Verify 'ENTER ACCOUNT INFORMATION' section is visible

tests.name: Fill Account Information and Create Account

steps.description: Select Title - Mr radio button
Enter password in the account information form
Select day of birth as 15
Select checkbox for Sign up for our newsletter!
Select checkbox for Receive special offers from our partners!
Enter First name in address information section
Enter Mobile Number in address information section
Click Create Account button to submit the registration form

tests.name: Verify Account Created and Login State, then Delete Account

steps.description: Verify 'ACCOUNT CREATED!' message is visible after successful registration
Click Continue button after account creation
Verify that 'Logged in as username' is visible in the navigation bar
Click Delete Account button to remove the newly created account
Verify 'ACCOUNT DELETED!' message is visible after deletion
Click Continue button after account deletion to return to home page

steps.expected: Signup / Login link visible, confirming user is logged out
steps.description: Verify user is redirected to home and is no longer logged in

tests.name: Negative - Signup with Already Registered Email

steps.description: Navigate to login/signup page
Verify the signup form is visible
Enter a name in the signup form
Enter an already registered email address
Click Signup button to attempt registration with duplicate email
Verify error message is shown for duplicate email registration

tests.name: Negative - Signup with Invalid Email Format

steps.description: Navigate to the login/signup page
Enter name in the signup form
Enter an invalid email format (missing @ and domain)
Click Signup button with invalid email

steps.expected: Browser-level HTML5 validation error on email field
steps.description: Verify that HTML5 validation prevents submission with invalid email format
Capture screenshot of validation error state for reporting

tests.name: Negative - Signup with Empty Name Field

steps.description: Navigate to the login/signup page
Verify the signup form is visible
Enter a valid email address but leave name field empty
Click Signup button with empty name field

steps.expected: Browser-level HTML5 validation error on name field
steps.description: Verify that HTML5 validation prevents submission when name is empty

steps.expected: ENTER ACCOUNT INFORMATION page should NOT appear
steps.description: Confirm user is not advanced to the account information step

issues.message: TC-REG-002 and TC-REG-003 rely on shared browser state from prior serial tests but use only 'page' fixture — if context resets between tests, state (cookies/session) will be lost and tests will fail
TC-REG-004 calls both homePage.navigate() and loginPage.navigate() — double navigation may override each other and is inconsistent with POM convention
TC-REG-005 and TC-REG-006 call loginPage.navigate() after homePage.navigate() but then interact directly with raw page locators instead of POM methods, violating the POM-only convention
TC-REG-002 uses raw page locators exclusively instead of POM fixture methods, violating the 'no raw page interactions' convention
TC-REG-003 uses raw page locators exclusively instead of POM fixture methods
expect.soft() used with await on textContent() inside expect.soft() — this pattern works but soft assertions on volatile async values can produce misleading failures
Hardcoded selector strings like "a[href='/delete_account']" and "a[href='/login']" are effectively hardcoded URLs/paths in test body
uniqueEmail is defined at describe scope but only used in TC-REG-001; TC-REG-002 and TC-REG-003 implicitly depend on it via shared browser state without referencing it — fragile coupling
page.screenshot() with hardcoded path 'test-results/TC-REG-005-invalid-email-validation.png' — hardcoded filesystem path in test body
TC-REG-002 should use a registrationPage or accountInfoPage POM rather than raw locators
Serial mode tests should share state via fixtures or storage state, not implicit browser session continuity
loginPage.navigate() is redundant after homePage.navigate() then homePage.goToLogin() — consider a single POM navigation method
Consider extracting duplicate annotation blocks into a helper or use tags via test metadata

suggestions: Create an AccountInfoPage POM to encapsulate TC-REG-002 form interactions
Use Playwright storageState or a shared fixture to pass session between serial tests explicitly
Replace loginPage.navigate() calls in negative tests with homePage.navigate() + homePage.goToLogin() for consistency
Remove hardcoded href selectors and expose them via POM methods
Use test.info().attachments for screenshots instead of hardcoded paths
Add a beforeEach hook in Happy Path describe to handle navigation setup shared across serial tests
Consider splitting serial tests into a single test with multiple steps to avoid implicit state dependency
